item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire, undermining Trump Iran peace efforts
item.description: Hezbollah rejected a new ceasefire in Lebanon and Israel refused to withdraw troops, dealing a blow to US efforts to halt fighting and forge peace with Iran.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian oil exports collapse to six-year low under US naval blockade
item.description: Iranian crude exports fell below 300,000 barrels per day in May, the lowest in at least six years, as the US naval blockade strangles Tehran's oil trade.

item.title: 🛢️ Saudi energy minister meets Russian counterpart as war disrupts OPEC+ output
item.description: Saudi Arabia's energy minister met Russian officials to discuss energy sector stabilization as the Iran and Ukraine wars cut output from the two largest OPEC+ producers.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China and US clash over Tiananmen anniversary as Taiwan demands reckoning
item.description: China denounced US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's Tiananmen comments as smearing, while Taiwan urged Beijing to face up to its history on the 37th anniversary.

item.title: 🏳️ UN peacekeeper killed in Lebanon shelling amid escalating conflict
item.description: A Serbian UN peacekeeper died from wounds sustained in mortar shelling near Marjayoun, the seventh peacekeeper killed in Lebanon since March.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 Pentagon Likely to Cancel Missile Sale to Germany Over Russia Fears
item.description: The US Defense Department is expected to cancel a missile sale to Germany, part of a broader American retrenchment from NATO alliance commitments as concerns grow about Russian responses.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Hezbollah Rejects US-Brokered Ceasefire, Israeli Strikes Kill Eight in Lebanon
item.description: Hezbollah dismissed a ceasefire agreement reached between Israel and the Lebanese government, as Israeli strikes continued in southern Lebanon killing eight people despite the Washington-announced deal.

item.title: 🇬🇧🕵️ MI6 Spy Chief Alex Younger Dies at 60
item.description: Alex Younger, who served as chief of Britains Secret Intelligence Service (MI6) from 2014 to 2020, has died at age 60. He led the agency during a period of significant global security challenges.

item.title: 🇱🇾 Libyan Protesters Block UN Refugee Office Over Migrant Crisis
item.description: Hundreds of demonstrators in Tripoli blocked the UN refugee agency office in protest against migrant policies, highlighting tensions over migration flows through North Africa.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇺🇦 Sweden Seizes Ship Suspected of Exporting Stolen Ukrainian Grain
item.description: Swedish authorities seized a vessel believed to be illegally exporting Ukrainian grain from Russian-occupied territories, marking the first case following a Ukrainian legal assistance request.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇷🇺 French jets scramble 11 times in a week to intercept Russian aircraft over Baltics
item.description: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times as part of NATO's Baltic Air Policing Mission, with France's armed forces spokesperson describing a higher-than-usual number of Russian "provocations" in the region.

item.title: ⚖️🏛️ Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty in classified documents case
item.description: John Bolton, who served as Trump's national security adviser and became a prominent critic, plans to plead guilty to mishandling classified documents and will pay a $2.25 million fine as part of a plea deal with federal prosecutors.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷⚖️ California tech CEO arrested for smuggling equipment to Iran's nuclear and military programs
item.description: US officials arrested dual US-Iranian citizen Jamshid Ghomi, accusing him of using his technology company to smuggle hundreds of tonnes of sophisticated computer equipment to Iran, including to the regime's nuclear and military establishment, violating US sanctions.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin says Russia will defeat Ukraine, signals readiness for peace compromises
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin declared Russia would defeat Ukraine while speaking at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, but also indicated readiness to make compromises for a Ukraine peace deal if Trump requests, showing dual messaging on the war's trajectory.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden's Gripen fighter jet faces combat test as Ukraine makes it backbone of air force
item.description: Ukraine's decision to make the Swedish Gripen the backbone of its air force puts the fighter jet to its ultimate test: confronting Russia in actual combat, the role it was designed for during the Cold War.

item.title: ⛽ Crimea tightens fuel rationing as Ukrainian drone strikes cause shortages
item.description: Russian-controlled Crimea suspended all cash sales of gasoline and halted new fuel coupons as the peninsula grapples with shortages linked to Ukrainian drone strikes on infrastructure.

item.title: 💥 Trump-arranged ceasefires failing to stop violence across Middle East
item.description: Residents of Gaza, south Lebanon, northern Israel and Kuwait all came under fire this week despite U.S.-arranged ceasefires supposedly in force, highlighting the fragility of American peace efforts in the region.

item.title: ⚖️ Nearly 100 Jan. 6 rioters granted clemency by Trump accused of new crimes
item.description: At least 97 people charged in connection with the Capitol riot have reoffended in the years since the attack, raising questions about the consequences of Trump's mass pardons.

item.title: 📦 Global supply chains under pressure from Middle East war, New York Fed reports
item.description: Federal Reserve data shows elevated global supply chain pressure in May due to the Middle East conflict, signaling inflation pressures will remain formidable for the foreseeable future.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russians panic as Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes on St. Petersburg have caused panic among Russia's business elite, who now directly witness how the Defense Ministry's actions affect their interests.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German prosecutors seek life sentence for Christmas market attacker
item.description: Prosecutors demanded life imprisonment for the Saudi man on trial over the 2024 car-ramming attack that killed six people at a German Christmas market.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU pledges €100M to strengthen Lebanese army amid ceasefire
item.description: The European Union approved a new 100-million-euro support package for Lebanon's military as it seeks to bolster the armed forces during a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Switzerland Deploys 4,000 Troops for G7 Summit Security
item.description: Switzerland will deploy around 4,000 troops to reinforce security as world leaders including President Trump gather for the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France (June 15-17). The proximity to the Swiss border requires close coordination between civilian and military authorities.

item.title: ⚖️ Israel Supreme Court Rules ICRC Must Visit Palestinian Prisoners
item.description: Israel's Supreme Court unanimously rejected a government policy banning the Red Cross from visiting Palestinian detainees, ruling that blocking visits contravened Israeli and international law. The policy had been in place since October 2023.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Mystery Structure at Scarborough Reef Appears Then Vanishes
item.description: Satellite imagery shows a structure appeared in late May at Scarborough Reef in the South China Sea - a hotspot of China-Philippines tensions - but was later removed. The reef is claimed by both nations and has been a flashpoint for maritime disputes.
